name: Great Blue Hole
description: A giant marine sinkhole off the coast of Belize. Famous for its perfectly circular shape and incredible stalactites at 124m depth.

name: SS Thistlegorm
description: British merchant navy ship sunk in 1941. One of the world's most famous wreck dives with WWII artifacts.

name: Barracuda Point
description: Famous for massive schools of barracuda and incredible biodiversity at Sipadan Island.

name: Cenote Dos Ojos
description: Part of the world's longest underwater cave system with crystal clear freshwater and stunning formations.

name: Molokini Crater
description: Crescent-shaped volcanic crater with pristine coral reefs and tropical fish in protected marine sanctuary.

name: Blue Corner
description: World-renowned drift dive site famous for shark encounters and strong currents.

name: Poor Knights Islands
description: Marine reserve with unique temperate water marine life and dramatic underwater landscapes.

name: Silfra Fissure
description: Dive between two tectonic plates in crystal clear glacial water. Unique geological experience.

name: Maldives Victory Wreck
description: Cargo ship wreck in shallow waters, perfect for underwater photography and marine life observation.

name: Liberty Wreck
description: WWII cargo ship wreck close to shore at Tulamben, accessible from the beach with incredible marine life.

name: Ras Mohammed
description: Famous for its shark and ray point with strong currents and pristine coral reefs in the Red Sea.

name: Richelieu Rock
description: Submerged pinnacle famous for whale shark encounters and diverse marine life in the Andaman Sea.
